stories.headline: Heavy fighting reported along eastern Ukraine front; civilian evacuations continue
stories.summary: Ukrainian and Russian forces exchanged artillery fire along multiple points of the eastern front. Regional officials reported continued civilian evacuations from frontline towns.

perspectives.keyFraming: Western European coverage emphasizes Russian advances and the strain on Ukrainian defenses.
perspectives.summary: Western outlets cite Ukrainian general staff briefings reporting incremental Russian gains. Coverage notes ammunition shortages and European officials saying additional aid is being expedited.

sources.title: Ukraine reports heavy fighting along eastern front

perspectives.keyFraming: Russian state media frames operations as steady advances against fortified positions.
perspectives.summary: Russian state outlets describe the operations as planned advances and report destruction of Ukrainian equipment. Independent verification is not available.

sources.title: Russian defense ministry reports advances on eastern front

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire coverage focuses on what is independently verifiable and the human cost.
perspectives.summary: Wires note battlefield claims from both sides cannot be independently verified and focus on confirmed civilian casualty figures and evacuation operations.

sources.title: Fighting intensifies in eastern Ukraine; civilians flee frontline towns

stories.headline: Congress reaches tentative deal on FY2027 budget framework
stories.summary: House and Senate leadership announced a tentative top-line agreement on the FY2027 budget framework, opening a path to avoid a partial government shutdown when current funding expires May 31.

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames the deal as Democrats forcing the GOP to back off proposed cuts to social programs.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning coverage emphasizes that Democratic negotiators preserved Medicaid expansion and rental-assistance funding, and that the deal walks back deeper non-defense discretionary cuts.

sources.title: Democrats hold line on social spending in budget framework deal

perspectives.keyFraming: Reports the deal as a procedural off-ramp rather than a policy victory for either side.
perspectives.summary: Wires describe the agreement as a top-line number with policy fights deferred. Appropriators have eleven legislative days to draft text and the deal is contingent on no new riders.

sources.title: US lawmakers strike tentative FY2027 budget framework, details pending

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames the deal as House Republicans giving up on the spending cuts they campaigned on.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning outlets argue the framework abandons deeper non-defense cuts conservatives demanded, and highlight Freedom Caucus members who say they'll vote against any package built on this top-line.

sources.title: Conservatives revolt as GOP leadership accepts higher spending baseline

stories.headline: Commerce Department finalizes new AI chip export rules
stories.summary: The Department of Commerce released final rules expanding restrictions on advanced AI accelerator exports to a broader list of countries. Industry groups raised concerns about implementation timing.

perspectives.keyFraming: Centers on the technical scope of the rules and the industry reaction.
perspectives.summary: Tech coverage details new license thresholds, country tiering, and a 60-day implementation window. Industry trade groups have asked for guidance on the 'unrestricted research collaboration' carve-out.

sources.title: US finalizes broader AI chip export controls

perspectives.keyFraming: Chinese state media frames the rules as US economic containment.
perspectives.summary: Chinese state outlets describe the rules as the latest in a multi-year US containment effort. Coverage highlights commentary calling the measures unilateral and damaging to global supply chains.

sources.title: US escalates chip-war with new export controls
